title: Adventurous Crispy Moroccan Chili Bean Stir-fry
description: An earthy and crispy Moroccan dish featuring chilli bean paste, deglazed with a low-sodium-friendly twist for an adventurous flavor profile.

ingredients.name: Crispy Potatoes (or other root vegetables), cut into cubes
Corn Starch or Corn Flour, for thickening the broth
Water, to thin out the sauce if needed

instructions: Heat a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat and add olive oil.
Add onions and garlic to the skillet, sauté until golden brown and fragrant.
Stir in chili bean paste and cook for about 2 minutes until fragrant.
If using red peppers, add them now and stir well.
Gradually pour in low-sodium chicken broth while continuously stirring to deglaze the pan, scraping up any tasty bits from the bottom.
Reduce heat to medium and mix in corn starch or flour. Stir until fully incorporated and sauce starts thickening slightly.
Add chopped crispy potatoes, reduce heat to low-medium, cover partially with a lid, and let simmer for about 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally to prevent burning.
If the sauce is too thin after this point, adjust seasoning and add water as needed. Taste test and adjust seasonings if necessary.
Garnish with fresh herbs like cilantro or parsley before serving.
Serve hot over steamed rice or over grilled bread for a delightful Moroccan-themed meal.
